questions.question: What is the primary purpose of packages in Java?
options: To organize related classes into namespaces and avoid naming conflicts
To encrypt Java source code files
To automatically compile multiple files at once
questions.answer: To organize related classes into namespaces and avoid naming conflicts

questions.question: How does a Java package relate to the file system?
options: Packages are stored in a single compressed file
Package names map directly to a folder/directory structure on disk
Packages have no relationship to the file system
Each package is stored in a separate hard drive partition
questions.answer: Package names map directly to a folder/directory structure on disk

questions.question: Given a class in the package com.example.utils, what must be the first non-comment line of that Java file?
What is the difference between 'import java.util.Scanner;' and 'import java.util.*;'?
options: There is no difference; they are identical
The first imports only the Scanner class; the second imports all classes in java.util
The first is invalid syntax; only wildcard imports are allowed
The second imports classes from java.util and all its sub-packages
questions.answer: The first imports only the Scanner class; the second imports all classes in java.util

questions.question: Which package is automatically imported into every Java program?
Which of the following classes does NOT belong to the java.lang package?
Why does the java.lang package not require an explicit import statement?
options: Because it contains only deprecated classes
Because it contains fundamental classes used by virtually every Java program
Because it is too large to import manually
Because it was the first package ever created in Java
questions.answer: Because it contains fundamental classes used by virtually every Java program

questions.question: Which of the following is a correct way to compile a class located at com/example/app/Main.java from the project root?
After compiling a packaged class, which command correctly runs com.example.app.Main?
What is the key difference between compiling and running a packaged Java class?
options: Compiling uses the package name with dots; running uses file paths with slashes
Compiling uses file paths with slashes; running uses the fully qualified class name with dots
There is no difference; both use the same syntax
Compiling requires the .class extension; running requires the .java extension
questions.answer: Compiling uses file paths with slashes; running uses the fully qualified class name with dots

questions.question: What does Integer.parseInt("42") return?
What does Integer.MAX_VALUE represent?
options: The maximum number of Integer objects that can be created
The largest possible value an int can hold (2147483647)
The maximum length of an integer's string representation
The maximum number of digits in an int
questions.answer: The largest possible value an int can hold (2147483647)

questions.question: Which method converts a String to a double value?
Which of the following are methods of the Math class? (Choose the best answer.)
What does System.currentTimeMillis() return?
options: The current date as a formatted String
The current time in seconds since midnight
The current time in milliseconds since January 1, 1970 (Unix epoch)
The number of milliseconds the program has been running
questions.answer: The current time in milliseconds since January 1, 1970 (Unix epoch)

questions.question: Which package does the Scanner class belong to?
What problem can occur when mixing scanner.nextInt() and scanner.nextLine()?
options: The program crashes with a NullPointerException
nextInt() leaves a newline in the buffer, causing nextLine() to read an empty string
nextLine() converts the input to an integer automatically
nextInt() cannot be called after nextLine() in the same program
questions.answer: nextInt() leaves a newline in the buffer, causing nextLine() to read an empty string

questions.question: What happens if you try to use Scanner without importing it?
options: The program runs but Scanner methods return null
Java automatically imports it from java.lang
You get a compilation error: 'cannot find symbol'
The program compiles but throws a runtime exception
questions.answer: You get a compilation error: 'cannot find symbol'

questions.question: Which package contains the LocalDate, LocalTime, and LocalDateTime classes?

Which classes need to be imported to get the ImportDetective program working?

ArrayList, Random, LocalDate, DecimalFormat
Which package does the DecimalFormat class belong to?
What is the default package in Java?
options: The unnamed package used when no package statement is declared
questions.answer: The unnamed package used when no package statement is declared

questions.question: A class in package com.internship.app needs to use a class in package com.internship.models. What must the app class do?
options: Nothing; classes in sub-packages of com.internship can see each other automatically
Import the class using an import statement such as 'import com.internship.models.Employee;'
Move both classes into the same package
Use the 'extends' keyword to inherit the models package
questions.answer: Import the class using an import statement such as 'import com.internship.models.Employee;'

questions.question: Which of the following is a valid String method from the java.lang.String class?
Does a wildcard import like 'import java.util.*;' also import classes from sub-packages such as java.util.stream?
options: Yes, wildcard imports include all sub-packages recursively
No, wildcard imports only import classes directly in that package, not sub-packages
Yes, but only if you also import the parent package java.*
It depends on the Java version being used
questions.answer: No, wildcard imports only import classes directly in that package, not sub-packages
